featuredBooks.author: Taylor Jenkins Reid

sellers.description: Wide selection of books across all genres
Curated book selections for every reader

books.title: Atomic Habits
books.author: James Clear
books.description: An easy and proven way to build good habits and break bad ones. Tiny changes, remarkable results.

books.title: The Psychology of Money
books.author: Morgan Housel
books.description: Timeless lessons on wealth, greed, and happiness. Doing well with money isn't about what you know.

books.title: Sapiens: A Brief History of Humankind
books.author: Yuval Noah Harari
books.description: From a renowned historian comes a groundbreaking narrative of humanity's creation and evolution.

books.title: The Midnight Library
books.author: Matt Haig
books.description: Between life and death there is a library, and within that library, the shelves go on forever.

books.title: Where the Crawdads Sing
books.author: Delia Owens
books.description: A coming-of-age mystery set in the marshes of North Carolina. A #1 New York Times bestseller.

books.title: The Silent Patient
books.author: Alex Michaelides
books.description: A shocking psychological thriller of a woman's act of violence against her husband.

books.title: Project Hail Mary
books.author: Andy Weir
books.description: From the bestselling author of The Martian comes a lone astronaut who must save the earth from disaster.

books.title: The Da Vinci Code
books.author: Dan Brown
books.description: A murder in the Louvre reveals a sinister plot to uncover a secret protected since the days of Christ.

books.title: Inferno
books.author: Dan Brown
books.description: Harvard professor Robert Langdon is drawn into a harrowing world centered on Dante's Inferno.

books.title: Become a Better You
books.author: Joel Osteen
books.description: Seven keys to improving your life every day from the bestselling inspirational author.

books.title: The Power of Dignity
books.author: Victoria Pratt
books.description: The visionary former judge's inspiring story of how dignity can transform lives and communities.

books.title: Dungeons & Dragons: The Legend of Drizzt
books.author: R.A. Salvatore
books.description: An epic fantasy adventure in the legendary world of Dungeons & Dragons.

books.title: Nineteen Eighty-Four
books.author: George Orwell
books.description: The chilling dystopian masterpiece about totalitarianism, surveillance, and the power of language.

books.title: Pride and Prejudice
books.author: Jane Austen
books.description: The beloved classic about love, class, and the stubborn Elizabeth Bennet and proud Mr. Darcy.

books.title: Book Lovers
books.author: Emily Henry
books.description: A witty, sparkling enemies-to-lovers romantic comedy from the bestselling author of Beach Read.

books.title: Klara and the Sun
books.author: Kazuo Ishiguro
books.description: The first novel by the Nobel laureate since Never Let Me Go — a new story of what it means to love.

books.title: The Alchemist
books.author: Paulo Coelho
books.description: A timeless fable about following your dream. An inspirational classic.

books.title: To Kill a Mockingbird
books.author: Harper Lee
books.description: The unforgettable novel of a childhood in a sleepy Southern town and the crisis of conscience that rocked it.

books.title: The Hobbit
books.author: J.R.R. Tolkien
books.description: The fantasy classic that introduced Bilbo Baggins, Gandalf, and the One Ring.

books.title: The Great Gatsby
books.author: F. Scott Fitzgerald
books.description: The authentic edition of the timeless masterpiece about the dark side of the American Dream.

books.title: The Catcher in the Rye
books.author: J.D. Salinger
books.description: The hero-narrator's voice of an adolescent at odds with the world, in a modern classic.

books.title: The Girl on the Train
books.author: Paula Hawkins
books.description: A psychological thriller that follows the intertwined lives of three women.

books.title: Normal People
books.author: Sally Rooney
books.description: A story of mutual fascination, friendship and a complex, enduring first love.

books.title: Macbeth
books.author: William Shakespeare
books.description: Shakespeare's dark tragedy of ambition, murder, and madness in medieval Scotland.

books.title: Lord of the Flies
books.author: William Golding
books.description: A group of schoolboys stranded on an island descends into savagery in this Nobel Prize-winning novel.

books.title: The Grapes of Wrath
books.author: John Steinbeck
books.description: Steinbeck's Pulitzer Prize-winning epic of the Great Depression and the Joad family's migration.

books.title: Jane Eyre
books.author: Charlotte Brontë
books.description: The timeless gothic romance of an orphaned governess and the brooding Mr. Rochester.

books.title: The Iliad
books.author: Homer
books.description: The ancient Greek epic of the Trojan War, Achilles, Hector, and the wrath of the gods.

books.title: Ulysses
books.author: James Joyce
books.description: Joyce's modernist masterpiece following Leopold Bloom through a single day in Dublin.

books.title: Crime and Punishment
books.author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
books.description: Dostoevsky's psychological masterpiece of guilt, redemption, and a desperate student's crime.

books.title: The Count of Monte Cristo
books.author: Alexandre Dumas
books.description: The epic tale of betrayal, imprisonment, and a meticulous revenge spanning decades.

books.title: Artificial Intelligence: A Guide for Thinking Humans
books.author: Melanie Mitchell
books.description: A penetrating assessment of what AI can and cannot do, from a leading computer scientist.

books.title: Superintelligence: Paths, Dangers, Strategies
books.author: Nick Bostrom
books.description: A foundational analysis of the future of machine intelligence and the existential questions it raises.

books.title: Introduction to Algorithms (CLRS)
books.author: Thomas H. Cormen et al.
books.description: The definitive algorithms textbook, covering a broad range of algorithms with rigor and clarity.

books.title: Artificial Intelligence: A Modern Approach
books.author: Stuart Russell, Peter Norvig
books.description: The most comprehensive introduction to the theory and practice of artificial intelligence.

books.title: Introduction to Machine Learning with Python
books.author: Andreas C. Müller, Sarah Guido
books.description: A practical guide to machine learning and deep learning with scikit-learn and TensorFlow.

books.title: Clean Code
books.author: Robert C. Martin
books.description: A handbook of agile software craftsmanship. Even bad code can function — but clean code endures.

books.title: The Pragmatic Programmer
books.author: Andy Hunt, Dave Thomas
books.description: Your journey to mastery. A timeless classic that teaches how to think like a pragmatic programmer.

books.title: The Clean Coder
books.author: Robert C. Martin
books.description: A code of conduct for professional programmers, on discipline, ethics, and craftsmanship.

books.title: Grokking Algorithms
books.author: Aditya Y. Bhargava
books.description: An illustrated guide for programmers and other curious people. Algorithms made beautifully simple.

books.title: Domain-Driven Design
books.author: Eric Evans
books.description: Tackling complexity in the heart of software. The foundational text on DDD.

books.title: Clean Architecture
books.author: Robert C. Martin
books.description: A craftsman's guide to software structure and design, from the author of Clean Code.

books.title: Refactoring
books.author: Martin Fowler
books.description: Improving the design of existing code. The definitive guide to refactoring, fully updated.

books.title: Building Microservices
books.author: Sam Newman
books.description: Designing fine-grained systems. A practical guide to the microservices architecture.

books.title: The Mythical Man-Month
books.author: Frederick P. Brooks
books.description: Essays on software engineering. Adding people to a late project makes it later — and other timeless wisdom.

books.title: Algorithms
books.author: Robert Sedgewick, Kevin Wayne
books.description: A comprehensive introduction to algorithms and data structures, with Java implementations.

books.title: Eloquent JavaScript
books.author: Marijn Haverbeke
books.description: A modern introduction to programming with JavaScript, from fundamentals to advanced topics.

books.title: Automate the Boring Stuff with Python
books.author: Al Sweigart
books.description: Practical programming for total beginners. Write programs that do in minutes what takes hours by hand.

books.title: How Linux Works
books.author: Brian Ward
books.description: What every superuser should know. A master class in understanding the Linux operating system.

books.title: Effective Java
books.author: Joshua Bloch
books.description: Best practices for the Java platform. The definitive guide to writing robust, maintainable Java.

books.title: Compilers: Principles, Techniques, and Tools
books.author: Alfred V. Aho et al.
books.description: The classic 'Dragon Book' on compiler construction, covering everything from lexing to optimization.

books.title: Watchmen
books.author: Alan Moore
books.description: A graphic novel masterpiece that redefined the superhero genre for a generation.

books.title: Saga, Vol. 1
books.author: Brian K. Vaughan
books.description: An epic space opera/fantasy from the award-winning creators of Y: The Last Man.

books.title: Maus I: A Survivor's Tale
books.author: Art Spiegelman
books.description: The Pulitzer Prize-winning graphic novel about the Holocaust, told with Jews as mice and Nazis as cats.

books.title: Persepolis
books.author: Marjane Satrapi
books.description: A memoir-in-comics of growing up during the Iranian Revolution.

books.title: The Walking Dead, Vol. 1
books.author: Robert Kirkman
books.description: The beginning of the acclaimed zombie survival epic.

books.title: Naruto, Vol. 1
books.author: Masashi Kishimoto
books.description: The legendary ninja manga. Uzumaki Naruto dreams of becoming the greatest Hokage.

books.title: Dragon Ball Z, Vol. 1
books.author: Akira Toriyama
books.description: The iconic shonen manga. Goku defends Earth against ever-more-powerful foes.

books.title: One Piece, Vol. 1
books.author: Eiichiro Oda
books.description: Monkey D. Luffy sets sail to become the King of the Pirates in the best-selling manga of all time.

books.title: Attack on Titan, Vol. 1
books.author: Hajime Isayama
books.description: Humanity lives behind giant walls to survive against man-eating Titans in this dark fantasy epic.

books.title: My Hero Academia, Vol. 1
books.author: Kohei Horikoshi
books.description: In a world of superpowers, a quirkless boy strives to become the greatest hero.

books.title: Tokyo Ghoul, Vol. 1
books.author: Sui Ishida
books.description: A dark horror fantasy about a college student turned half-ghoul, struggling with his new hunger.

books.title: Sailor Moon, Vol. 1
books.author: Naoko Takeuchi
books.description: The magical-girl classic. Usagi transforms into Sailor Moon to protect the world from evil.

books.title: World Trigger, Vol. 1
books.author: Daisuke Ashihara
books.description: Earth is invaded by mysterious creatures from another dimension — defended by the Border agency.

books.title: Avengers Epic Collection
books.author: Gerry Conway
books.description: Earth's Mightiest Heroes assemble in this classic Marvel collection.

books.title: Ms. Marvel, Vol. 1
books.author: G. Willow Wilson
books.description: Kamala Khan, a Muslim teen from Jersey City, discovers her shape-shifting superpowers.

books.title: गोदान
books.author: Premchand
books.description: Premchand's magnum opus — a poignant story of rural India, poverty, and the quiet heroism of a farmer named Hori. Widely regarded as the first major Hindi novel of the 20th century.

books.title: रश्मिरथी
books.author: Ramdhari Singh Dinkar
books.description: An epic narrative poem on the life of Karna from the Mahabharata — Dinkar's ode to the unsung hero. A cornerstone of modern Hindi literature.

books.title: गीतांजलि
books.author: Rabindranath Tagore
books.description: Tagore's Nobel-winning collection of spiritual poems, originally written in Bengali and translated across India. Verses that still move readers a century later.

books.title: कामायनी
books.author: Jaishankar Prasad
books.description: A modern Hindi epic poem weaving philosophy, love, and mythology — Prasad's masterwork and one of the great landmarks of the Chhayavaad movement.

books.title: मैला आंचल
books.author: Phanishwarnath Renu
books.description: A vivid portrait of a Bihar village — its people, its caste divisions, and its slow awakening. Considered the first major Hindi 'Aanchalik Upanyas' (regional novel).

books.title: चित्रलेखा
books.author: Bhagwaticharan Verma
books.description: A celebrated Hindi novel that weaves the personal and the political through the life of Chitralekha — set against the non-cooperation movement of 1921.

books.title: तीसरी कसम
books.author: Phanishwarnath Renu
books.description: Renu's celebrated short story of a bullock-cart driver and the nautanki dancer he falls for — the basis for the iconic Shailendra film. A small, perfect gem of Hindi prose.

books.title: निर्मला
books.author: Munshi Premchand
books.description: Premchand's poignant novel of a young girl trapped between an abusive husband and a society that offers no escape — a landmark in the Hindi feminist novel.

books.title: आषाढ़ का एक दिन
books.author: Mohan Rakesh
books.description: Rakesh's landmark Hindi play that broke with stage tradition — a story of the poet Kalidasa, romantic longing, and the gulf between art and life. A turning point in modern Hindi theatre.

books.title: अंधायुग
books.author: Dharamvir Bharati
books.description: Bharati's post-independence play set in the aftermath of the Mahabharata war — a meditation on guilt, justice, and the moral collapse that follows victory.

books.title: मधुशाला
books.author: Harivansh Rai Bachchan
books.description: Bachchan's most famous poetry collection — 135 quatrains built around the metaphor of a tavern. A best-seller in independent India and a touchstone of Hindi poetry.

books.title: उर्वशी
books.author: Ramdhari Singh Dinkar
books.description: Dinkar's lyrical epic — the story of the apsara Urvashi and the mortal king Pururavas, told with the grandeur of classical Hindi poetry.

books.title: सूरज का सातवाँ घोड़ा
books.author: Dharamvir Bharati
books.description: Bharati's most celebrated novel — the seven horses of the sun are a metaphor for the seven stages of a single night in which a young man's life quietly pivots.

books.title: झूठा सच
books.author: Yashpal
books.description: Yashpal's sweeping novel of the Partition — millions displaced, friendships broken, and the question of what 'truth' means in the middle of a catastrophe.

books.title: Cien años de soledad
books.author: Gabriel García Márquez
books.description: The multi-generational saga of the Buendía family in the fictional town of Macondo — García Márquez's masterpiece and a cornerstone of magical realism.

books.title: Don Quijote
books.author: Miguel de Cervantes
books.description: The adventures of the self-styled knight Don Quixote and his squire Sancho Panza — widely regarded as the first modern novel and a founding text of Western literature.

books.title: Veinte poemas de amor y una canción desesperada
books.author: Pablo Neruda
books.description: Neruda's most beloved poetry collection — twenty love poems and one song of despair that helped earn him the Nobel Prize in Literature.

books.title: La casa de los espíritus
books.author: Isabel Allende
books.description: Allende's debut novel — three generations of the Trueba family unfold against the backdrop of Chile's political upheavals in the 20th century.

books.title: El amor en los tiempos del cólera
books.author: Gabriel García Márquez
books.description: A love story that spans fifty-one years, nine months, and four days — García Márquez's tender meditation on aging, memory, and devotion.

books.title: Crónica de una muerte anunciada
books.author: Gabriel García Márquez
books.description: A novella told with the precision of a journalism investigation — everyone knew the murder was coming, yet no one stopped it. A short, devastating classic.

books.title: La sombra del viento
books.author: Carlos Ruiz Zafón
books.description: Zafón's Barcelona-set mystery — a boy discovers a forgotten book in the Cemetery of Forgotten Books and is drawn into a decades-old secret. The first volume of 'El cementerio de los libros olvidados'.

books.title: Pedro Páramo
books.author: Juan Rulfo
books.description: Rulfo's short, hallucinatory novel of a man searching for his father in the ghost-town of Comala — the book that redefined what Latin American fiction could be.

books.title: Rayuela
books.author: Julio Cortázar
books.description: Cortázar's experimental masterpiece — a novel you can read in two orders, with chapters that can be skipped, shuffled, or followed to two distinct endings. Pure literary play.

books.title: Ficciones
books.author: Jorge Luis Borges
books.description: Borges's classic short-story collection — labyrinths, mirrors, infinite libraries, and the philosophical fictions that reshaped 20th-century literature.

books.title: Como agua para chocolate
books.author: Laura Esquivel
books.description: Esquivel's magical-realist novel of food and passion — each chapter is a recipe, each recipe a story, set on the Mexican border at the turn of the 20th century.

books.title: El túnel
books.author: Ernesto Sábato
books.description: Sábato's existential thriller — a painter confesses to a murder he both did and did not commit. A short, claustrophobic classic of Latin American letters.

books.title: Los detectives salvajes
books.author: Roberto Bolaño
books.description: Bolaño's sprawling novel of two poets in search of a vanished Mexican writer — a picaresque, heartbreaking ode to youth, literature, and the long aftermath of the avant-garde.

books.title: La fiesta del chivo
books.author: Mario Vargas Llosa
books.description: Vargas Llosa's novel of the Trujillo dictatorship in the Dominican Republic — a reconstruction of power, terror, and a dictator's final afternoon.

cartItems.title: Sapiens: A Brief History of Humankind
cartItems.author: Yuval Noah Harari

testimonials.name: Sarah Johnson
testimonials.text: My Book Store has completely transformed my reading experience. The curated recommendations are always spot-on, and their delivery is lightning fast!

testimonials.name: Michael Chen
testimonials.text: I've been ordering books for our club for years. The selection is unmatched, and their customer service is exceptional. Truly a book lover's paradise.

testimonials.name: Emma Williams
testimonials.text: As a student, I appreciate the academic section and the student discounts. Found rare editions I couldn't find anywhere else. Highly recommended!
